steps.title: Recognize the linear growth model structure
steps.content:
The equation $0.2t + 3 = 6$ represents a linear growth model with the pattern:
$$\text{(rate of growth)} \times \text{(time in months)} + \text{(starting length)} = \text{(final length)}$$

steps.title: Determine the units of 0.2 and match to the answer
steps.content: Since $0.2$ multiplies $t$ (in months) in an equation about lengths measured in inches, the product $0.2 \cdot t$ must yield inches. Therefore, $0.2$ has units of inches per month—the amount of hair that grows each month. This is exactly what choice C describes: the length, in inches, Antwon's hair will grow each month. That matches choice C — C is correct.
